name: Tlaxcala (Mexico)

colors.symbolism: the field and details of the arms

description: A white field charged with the elaborate state coat of arms of Tlaxcala in red, blue, yellow, green and grey, centred.
history: Tlaxcala has no separately legislated state flag; the design shown — the state coat of arms on a white field — is in de facto use to represent the state.

features.symbolism: The state's heraldic emblem, charged with elements drawn from the colonial coat of arms granted by Charles V and from indigenous Tlaxcalan symbols.

The arms combine the heraldic charges granted to Tlaxcala by Charles V in 1535 in recognition of its alliance with Hernán Cortés against the Aztec Empire (including the castle, palms and the letters of the Catholic monarchs) with indigenous Tlaxcalan symbols, marking the state's foundational role in the conquest and Christianisation of New Spain.
